title: Clarify the commit-to-done workflow contract in canonical and generated `.rules`
summary: Align the machine-facing workflow contract so agents can reliably distinguish between successful commit completion and terminal task completion. This ticket updates the canonical `.rules` contract and the generated rules surfaces so they explicitly state that commit helpers may leave a transaction in non-terminal `committed` with `next_action = tx.end.done`, and that terminal success still requires an explicit `ops_end_task(status="done")` transition.
outputs: Updated canonical `.rules` text that explicitly describes `committed` as non-terminal and requires explicit terminal closure
Synchronized generated rules content in `workflow_rules.py` and `workflow_rules_fallback.txt`
Bootstrap-generated workspace `.rules` content that matches the clarified commit/finalize contract
acceptance_criteria: The canonical `.rules` explicitly states that successful commit helpers may leave the active transaction in `committed` and that this state is non-terminal.
The canonical `.rules` explicitly states that terminal success still requires `tx.end.done`, typically through `ops_end_task(status="done")`.
The canonical `.rules` explicitly instructs agents to use canonical transaction status and `next_action` to determine whether follow-up lifecycle completion is still required.
The generated rules content derived from `workflow_rules.py` and `workflow_rules_fallback.txt` matches the clarified canonical contract for commit, committed, and done transitions.
The rules written into initialized workspaces by `zed-agentops-init.sh` remain contract-identical to the canonical rules in this area.
The clarified rules preserve the current two-stage lifecycle model rather than collapsing `committed` and `done` into a single state.
tasks: Revise the work loop section of the canonical `.rules` so it explicitly distinguishes commit success from terminal task completion.
Add explicit wording that `commit_if_verified` and `repo_commit` may advance the transaction only to `committed`, not necessarily to terminal `done`.
Add explicit wording that if canonical `next_action` is `tx.end.done`, the agent must complete the lifecycle with `ops_end_task(status="done")`.
Update `workflow_rules.py` so the generated canonical rules reflect the clarified commit/finalize contract.
Update `workflow_rules_fallback.txt` so fallback-generated workspace rules remain synchronized with the canonical rules.
Confirm that the rules distribution path used by `zed-agentops-init.sh` continues to produce workspace `.rules` content matching the clarified contract.
notes: This ticket is about machine-facing workflow contract clarity, not README wording.
The primary consumer of this change is an AI agent that follows `.rules` and adjusts behavior based on tool responses and canonical transaction state.
The ticket should preserve the existing transaction model in which `committed` is a meaningful non-terminal state and `done` remains a distinct terminal state.
Because initialized workspaces consume generated rules rather than repository prose directly, synchronization across `.rules`, `workflow_rules.py`, and `workflow_rules_fallback.txt` is mandatory.
